author: @bikerfirefarter7280
text:
Just one point, about the 45-seond to 1-minute mark, 'why bother? we're going to be here!' That is only correct for a tiny percent. The majority of people I know/meet, have the 'fire up another petrol-engine' mentality (that's MOST people) and they sincerely believe they will transcend into paradise when their mortal body gives out. They have no care about the future beyond that, it's not their problem. Most say/believe 'climate-change/pollution etc is NOTHING to do with them'; the notion is 'I don't make plastic/chemicals/petrol etc so it's not my fault; I put all my 'rubbish' in the bin'! (then apparently magic occurs with their plastic/chemical/fumes stuff). And a thousand other excuses/deflections that they use rather than face reality.
Those are the people you need to convince.  Good luck with that challenge.   I wont be holding my breath waiting.

author: @jamest.5001
text: When it comes to the climate, and basically everything, even the earth, I look at like I'm going to be here forever! And I'm going to always be poor, so I need to take care of everything! We really ,really, absolutely need to be much more efficient, and cut carbon production!  We are making Co2 like we are being paid to do so! By the atom!  You can't tell me after a few hundred years of making and studying machines and equipment, someone hasn't found atleast an idea of something that works, a 'Better way' it must me possible, we just need to get the rich to stop getting richer! Stop the coal and oil, it's supply and demand, if we stop demanding they stop supplying! They are like drug dealers! They are selling what's keeping us down, and killing us! With floods and fires, hurricanes, tornadoes happening every few weeks, taking out whole towns! We act like it's normal, half the Forrest are burning, but you don't see it on the news, how much has burned, and it's our fault! They don't show how much destruction we caused, because they fear we will stop beating down their door for more coal and oil!!! I love fast cars and bikes, but we must find a better way, or we are not going to have a 'here' to save, or we aren't going to be here. mother nature is going to get rid of us!  No one is going to do it for us, we must save ourselves!

author: @benniedonald

author: @PressToDigitate
text: That was great, albeit too short.  We recently watched all of "Penelope Keith's Hidden Villages" on Amazon, which was Wonderful (apart from some dodgy sound on a couple of episodes).  It would be magnificent to watch you tour the industrial & technological historic sites of the UK, from the first looms and mills and mines and steam engines/locomotion, to Radar, to Bletchley Park & Colossus, to Whittle's Turbine, to Black Arrow, to Skylon and the new Scottish Spaceport.  The Industrial Revolution was born through British Technology, and it could do with a modern retrospective, and nobody - save, perhaps, the incomparable James Burke - could do a better job of it than our favorite British Battery Boffin.

author: @team2001
text: I want to plant a little seed in your mind regarding to harness of free electricity from an iron ball elevated at 3 wooden poles like 2 meters off the ground. between the iron ball and the magnetic field of the earth... What is missing to generate a usable current we can use? The energy is there we just have to put in something between that can make use of the power already there

replies.author: @bikerfirefarter7280
replies.text:
Bugger all 'energy' available from the iron-ball and Earth's magnetic field.
You could use the gravitational potential energy of the ball by letting it turn a generator as it descends, but you'd get far less out than the energy you put in lifting it up.
Dim wit.

author: @noscreadur
text: Croeso iawn Robert. As a long-standing viewer of your videos and renewable energy engineer, it's great to see you in my home town! It's a special place. Most of the people I kick about with are involved with either sustainable energy or festival event facilitation. Suffice to say, going to bed at six in the afternoon is pretty much unheard of. CAT is well worth a visit for anyone interested in getting some experiments and projects fired up.

author: @lightcapmath2777

author: @scotttovey
text:
I don't have a problem with developing technologies that have the potential of producing clean energy.

I have a major problem with anyone or group of evil carcasses that ban the production of petroleum, and mandates the implementation of  unreasonable and unworkable energy policies that do more harm than good; over this lie that if we don't fix this problem we have never had control over; yesterday.

If the environmental wackos had followed reasonable, step by step policies that were workable over the years; we would be further ahead today when it comes to the use of clean energy. But they demand that we flip a switch and just turn off petroleum. This is how you know that they can neither be trusted and should be put in their graves. 

As the State of Texas learned last winter, the policies of the leftist environmental wackos; murder people.

When the fourth angel pours his vial upon the sun, and men are scorched with great heat, what are they going to do?

Say we need to snuff out the sun?

Revelation 16:8: And the fourth angel poured out his vial upon the sun; and power was given unto him to scorch men with fire.
9: And men were scorched with great heat, and blasphemed the name of God, which hath power over these plagues: and they repented not to give him glory.

I will always advocate for using the petroleum economy to birth and bring forth technologies that produce clean energy, and only allow the market to transition the economy from petroleum to clean energy. Doing otherwise is reckless, dangerous, unwise and economic suicide.

author: @CaminasDrummers
text: The climate is already screwed. Existing emissions are enough to take us to +2.6-3.8C above pre-industrial. We need to correct the energy imbalance, the MEER:ReflEction project is a way to do that.

author: @matthewellisor5835
text:
Mate, I might not know you more than to exchange a few words here from time to time.

We aren't likely to ever meet. (That truth does offer a moment of sadness.)

I write to tell you that your encouraging words, hopeful nature, excitement at "giving it a go" and the sharing your travels has been a great help to me while I seem to be a bit off from good health. (No need for worry at that bit. While I breathe, I hope.)

Thank you! I truly mean it; thank you.

author: @timredd
text: Machynlleth, I visited the centre for alternative technology about 30 years ago on two occasions. I haven't revisited since those days, but some useful information was gleaned and I'll always remember the heat exchanger panels giving heated water from sunlight, the thermal insulated walls ( 3 feet thick ) of the one radiator heated cottage. Also the wind turbines and solar generator electricity demo's.
